anti_claims: This organ JSON seed does not flip source authority away from core/organ_atlas.json and core/organ_registry.json.
Required paper/mechanism/code links prove declared structural references only, not runtime correctness or release readiness.
Selective concept, principle, axiom, and sibling-organ edges are not inferred from prose, specialty, family, or evidence class.
Absent selective organ edges are residual pressure, not evidence that no neighbours exist.
Generated markdown, graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

selective doctrine neighbours not named by atlas source rows
omission_receipt.reason: Organ instance preserves public-safe atlas/registry fields. Required links resolve only when source ids or paths resolve; selective law/concept/principle edges remain residual unless source evidence names ids.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Populate missing paper/mechanism/code links from source evidence, then bind concept/principle/axiom/wires_to edges only when ids are named.

organ_payload.claim_ceiling: It validates only a declared, synthetic governed-mutation contract and authorizes no live cloud/account action, standing credentials, source or irreversible mutation, policy-after-execution, hidden votes, provider calls, benchmark-score claims, or release.

source_atlas_row.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm a mutation-authorization claim is backed by replayable proof, pre-execution visible verdicts, logged-and-reversible side effects, and that the eight rigged failure cases (standing credential, policy-after-execution, hidden vote, live cloud credential, irreversible mutation, unlogged side effect, consensus-without-evidence, final-answer-only) are all caught.
source_atlas_row.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only a declared, synthetic governed-mutation contract and authorizes no live cloud/account action, standing credentials, source or irreversible mutation, policy-after-execution, hidden votes, provider calls, benchmark-score claims, or release.

code_loci.role: Runtime and receipt-writing locus for public governed-mutation authorization validation, exported bundle source-module digest checks, negative-case enforcement, authority ceilings, and body-free public receipts.

source_atlas_row.human_gloss: Replays a make-believe example of "should this change be allowed to run?" and shows, step by step, why each proposed action was permitted. All three actions (a look-only inspection, a small config write, and an undo of that write) had to carry proof evidence and two visible policy approvals before anything was admitted; on top of that, the two actions that actually change something (the config write and the undo) also had to show a logged record of the change and a matching undo receipt. Just holding a password or credential is never treated as permission, and nothing here touches a real account or makes any real change.

source_atlas_row.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas); bundle mode also imports copied non-secret macro bodies, not other named organs. Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
